reference_values: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.
Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.
IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Confirm hyperprolactinemia with repeat fasting morning prolactin when mild or discordant; exclude pregnancy, hypothyroidism, kidney disease, medications, chest wall causes, and macroprolactin.
Prolactin in the 15-20 ng/mL range generally excludes hyperprolactinemia in many assays, but use local reference intervals.
Obtain pituitary MRI for persistent unexplained prolactin elevation or mass-effect symptoms.
Assess hypogonadism, fertility goals, visual fields, macroadenoma size, pregnancy, and apoplexy symptoms.
Confirm that Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.

Order the first-line tests in sequence: Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant; Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function; Pituitary MRI after persistent unexplained elevation or mass symptoms.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Use dopamine agonist therapy, usually cabergoline when appropriate, for most symptomatic prolactinomas or macroadenomas.
Monitor prolactin response, tumor size, vision, gonadal recovery, and medication tolerance.
Use surgery or radiation when medication is not tolerated, ineffective, or urgent decompression is required.
Manage pregnancy with specialist plan, especially macroadenoma or visual symptoms.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor), choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

source_thresholds.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L

Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm

exact_data_needed: Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?
When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
How long has conception been attempted, are cycles ovulatory/regular, and have partner semen analysis or prior fertility evaluations found abnormalities?
Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms?
weight when dosing depends on it
Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms.
Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.
Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function.
Pituitary MRI after persistent unexplained elevation or mass symptoms.
Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.

Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants.
Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.
Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.

root.description: Activate when structured or extractable context matches Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) triggers or the clinician selects workup prolactinoma_v1.

endpoint_missing_context.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) is absent or cannot be extracted.

prolactinoma_concurrent_data_bundle.description: Obtain Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

prolactinoma_classification_decision.description: Classify Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) against cited thresholds and condition-specific rules: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L: >ULN, <25 ug/L | Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm: >250 ug/L, >500 ug/L, >10 mm | Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.: 20 ng/mL, 25 ng/mL | Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant.: 20 ng/mL, 25 ng/mL | Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function.: 20 ng/mL, 25 ng/mL

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L

criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm

children.edgeLabel: Selected Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) workup or documented findings: Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?; How long has conception been attempted, are cycles ovulatory/regular, and have partner semen analysis or prior fertility evaluations found abnormalities?; Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms?; Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant.

criteria.description: Obtain Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

children.action: Obtain together: Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant.; Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function.; Pituitary MRI after persistent unexplained elevation or mass symptoms.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Prolactin/pituitary mass safety tests: repeat fasting prolactin with macroprolactin, medication, pregnancy, TSH, and renal context, obtain pituitary MRI for persistent or mass-effect concern, and check gonadal/adrenal/thyroid axes for macroadenoma.; Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible.; Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated.; Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.; Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Document mental status; serum prolactin; assay ULN; venipuncture stress; pregnancy/lactation status; medication list; prolactin; pituitary MRI size; drug causes such as risperidone/metoclopramide.

children.label: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): emergency escalation or monitored-care disposition when danger thresholds are met
children.edgeLabel: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy.; Measure blood pressure; >ULN; <25 ug/L; >250 ug/L; Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.

criteria.description: Escalate Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) when any condition-specific danger feature, high-risk cutoff, unstable physiology, or monitored-care disposition rule is present.

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

children.action: Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy.; Measure blood pressure. Use dopamine agonist therapy, usually cabergoline when appropriate, for most symptomatic prolactinomas or macroadenomas.; Monitor prolactin response, tumor size, vision, gonadal recovery, and medication tolerance.; Use surgery or radiation when medication is not tolerated, ineffective, or urgent decompression is required.; Manage pregnancy with specialist plan, especially macroadenoma or visual symptoms.; Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

children.action: Use dopamine agonist therapy, usually cabergoline when appropriate, for most symptomatic prolactinomas or macroadenomas.; Monitor prolactin response, tumor size, vision, gonadal recovery, and medication tolerance.; Use surgery or radiation when medication is not tolerated, ineffective, or urgent decompression is required.; Manage pregnancy with specialist plan, especially macroadenoma or visual symptoms.; Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

children.edgeLabel: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: >ULN; <25 ug/L; >250 ug/L; >500 ug/L; Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Use dopamine agonist therapy, usually cabergoline when appropriate, for most symptomatic prolactinomas or macroadenomas.

criteria.description: Stop, narrow, taper, or de-escalate Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) treatment only when objective response, diagnosis certainty, and cited stopping criteria support it.

criteria_options.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

criteria_options.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

criteria_options.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

parallel_actions: Use dopamine agonist therapy, usually cabergoline when appropriate, for most symptomatic prolactinomas or macroadenomas.
Monitor prolactin response, tumor size, vision, gonadal recovery, and medication tolerance.
Use surgery or radiation when medication is not tolerated, ineffective, or urgent decompression is required.
Manage pregnancy with specialist plan, especially macroadenoma or visual symptoms.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 1.1

guideline_cutoffs.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

clinical_criteria.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
clinical_criteria.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

clinical_criteria.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy.; Measure blood pressure; >ULN; <25 ug/L; >250 ug/L; Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.

Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) mimic/exclusion better fits: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.; Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Hyperprolactinemia diagnosis: single prolactin above assay ULN confirms when drawn without excessive venipuncture stress; normal values are generally <25 ug/L
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends a single prolactin measurement above the assay upper limit to diagnose hyperprolactinemia if venipuncture stress is avoided; normal values are generally lower than 25 ug/L.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Prolactinoma probability by prolactin level: >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma; >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroadenoma >10 mm
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society notes prolactin >250 ug/L usually indicates prolactinoma and >500 ug/L is diagnostic of macroprolactinoma; macroprolactinomas are >10 mm and usually have prolactin >250 ug/L.
clinical_cutoff_criteria.source_section: Diagnosis recommendation 1.1 evidence and Pituitary Society diagnostic algorithm

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Hook-effect exclusion: large pituitary tumor with unexpectedly mild prolactin elevation needs repeat assay after 1:100 dilution
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: When a large macroadenoma has normal or only mildly elevated prolactin, Endocrine Society recommends serial dilution; a 1:100 serum dilution can overcome the hook effect.

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
